title: Add 'What's on Your Mind' post/thought upload page

requirements.text: Add a backend data model for posts/thoughts with fields: postId (UUID or Nat), authorPrincipal, textContent (String), optionalMediaBlob (optional blob for image/video), optionalMediaMimeType (optional Text), createdAt (Int timestamp), and likes (Nat). Store posts in a stable map keyed by postId.

quotes: Add page what's on your mind where they can upload posts and thoughts and their content

acceptanceCriteria: Post data type is defined with all required fields
Posts are stored in stable memory so they survive upgrades
Each post has a unique identifier

requirements.text: Expose the following backend update and query functions for posts: createPost (takes textContent and optional media blob + mimeType, stores post under caller's principal), deletePost (caller can only delete their own posts), getAllPosts (returns all posts sorted by createdAt descending), getPostsByUser (returns posts for a given principal), and likePost (increments like count for a post).

quotes: upload posts and thoughts and their content

acceptanceCriteria: createPost stores a post and returns the new postId
deletePost rejects callers who are not the post author
getAllPosts returns posts in reverse chronological order
getPostsByUser filters posts by a given principal
likePost increments the like counter and returns the new count

requirements.text: Add React Query hooks in useQueries.ts for all post operations: useGetAllPosts, useGetPostsByUser, useCreatePost (mutation), useDeletePost (mutation), and useLikePost (mutation). Invalidate the posts query cache on successful create, delete, or like mutations.

quotes: upload posts and thoughts and their content

acceptanceCriteria: useGetAllPosts fetches all posts and caches them under a 'posts' query key
useCreatePost mutation calls the backend and invalidates the posts cache on success
useDeletePost and useLikePost mutations also invalidate the posts cache
useGetPostsByUser accepts a principal parameter and fetches that user's posts

requirements.text: Create a new page component at frontend/src/pages/WhatsOnYourMindPage.tsx. The page should include: a prominent heading 'What's on Your Mind?', a text area for typing thoughts or post content (max ~500 characters with a live character counter), an optional media attachment input supporting images and videos (displayed as a preview before posting), a 'Post' submit button (disabled when text is empty and no media is attached, and shows a loading state while posting), and a feed of all posts displayed below the composer in reverse chronological order. Each post card must show: the author's display name and avatar (fetched via existing profile hooks), the post text, an optional media preview, a like button with count, the post timestamp, and a delete button (visible only to the post's author).

quotes: Add page what's on your mind where they can upload posts and thoughts and their content

acceptanceCriteria: Page renders a composer area with a text area and optional media upload
Character counter updates in real time and prevents submission over the limit
Submitted post appears immediately in the feed after creation
Post cards show author avatar, display name, text, optional media, like count, and timestamp
Only the post's author sees the delete button; clicking it removes the post after confirmation
Like button increments the count optimistically and syncs with the backend
Page is accessible when the user is not logged in (read-only feed; composer hidden or disabled)

requirements.text: Register a new route '/mind' for the WhatsOnYourMindPage in frontend/src/App.tsx and add a navigation link labeled 'What's on Your Mind' (or a short variant like 'Mind') to the sticky header in frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx, alongside the existing Feed and Upload links.

quotes: Add page what's on your mind

acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to /mind renders the WhatsOnYourMindPage
The header navigation includes a link to the /mind route
Active route link is visually highlighted consistently with existing nav links

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
All Motoko logic must remain in the single backend/main.mo actor
Reuse existing profile picture and display name resolution patterns already present in useQueries.ts
nonGoals: Separate notification system for likes or comments on posts
